description: Test corpus for the database-security skill. This skill is schema-driven: no deterministic gate scanner judges these fixtures, so each fixture is hand-labeled against the SKILL.md ALWAYS/NEVER rules — 'detect' means the code is genuinely SQL-injectable or runs as a DB superuser, 'ignore' means it uses parameterized queries / an allowlist / a least-privilege user.

fixtures.reason: Concatenates user input directly into the SQL string, violating NEVER 'Concatenate user input into SQL'. CWE-89 (SQL Injection); a name of "' OR '1'='1" dumps the table.
Passes the value as a bound parameter (psycopg2 %s placeholder, not a format string), satisfying ALWAYS 'Use parameterized queries'. No CWE-89 exposure — the driver escapes correctly.

Uses the ORM raw query method with template-literal interpolation of user input, violating NEVER 'Use ORM raw query methods with f-string interpolation'. CWE-89 (SQL Injection) via the term parameter.
Uses Sequelize's named-replacement binding so the term is passed as a parameter, satisfying ALWAYS 'Use the ORM's safe query API'. The LIKE wildcards are part of the bound value; no CWE-89 exposure.

Interpolates a user-supplied identifier (sort column) — which can't be parameterized — straight into SQL with no allowlist, violating ALWAYS 'Validate that identifier columns ... come from a hard-coded allowlist'. CWE-89 (SQL Injection) via the ORDER BY clause.

Constrains the sort column to a hard-coded allowlist before building the query, satisfying ALWAYS 'Validate that identifier columns ... come from a hard-coded allowlist'. No attacker-controlled value reaches the SQL; no CWE-89 exposure.
